input: A company is developing a new pulse oximeter. Recommend the regulatory pathway and identify potential predicate devices for a 510(k) submission.

expected_details.predicate_strategy: identify substantially equivalent legally marketed devices

input: Analyze the patent cliff for Humira (adalimumab). When do key patents expire? What biosimilars are available?

expected_details.key_patent_expiry: 2023 (US) — patent thicket strategy extended exclusivity from 2016 to 2023

input: A sponsor has Phase 2 data for a novel kinase inhibitor in a rare sarcoma (ORR 42%, N=60). Recommend a regulatory strategy for FDA approval.

expected_details.pathway: accelerated approval based on ORR (surrogate endpoint)
expected_details.confirmatory_trial: randomized Phase 3 with OS or PFS as primary endpoint

input: Check the recall history and current safety status of ranitidine (Zantac). What happened and why?

expected_details.mechanism: ranitidine molecule is inherently unstable → forms NDMA at elevated temperatures
expected_details.fda_action: requested all manufacturers withdraw ranitidine from market (April 2020)
expected_details.scope: all OTC and prescription ranitidine products

input: A startup has developed an AI-based diagnostic tool that uses retinal imaging to detect diabetic retinopathy. No predicate exists. Recommend the pathway.

expected_details.pathway: De Novo classification (novel device, no predicate, low-to-moderate risk)
expected_details.precedent: IDx-DR (first FDA-authorized AI diagnostic device, De Novo 2018)
expected_details.alternative_pathway: 510(k) using IDx-DR as predicate if substantially equivalent

input: A generic company wants to launch a generic version of Eliquis (apixaban). What are the patent and exclusivity barriers?

expected_details.active_patents: multiple patents including composition of matter and methods of use
expected_details.litigation_risk: patent infringement lawsuit likely within 45 days of ANDA filing

input: Does osimertinib (Tagrisso) require a companion diagnostic? If so, which one?

expected_details.requirement_type: FDA mandated — prescribing requires EGFR mutation confirmation
expected_details.alternative_tests: FDA-approved NGS panels (FoundationOne CDx) also approved

input: Is there an interchangeable biosimilar for insulin glargine (Lantus)? What does interchangeability mean practically?

expected_details.interchangeable_biosimilar: insulin glargine-yfgn (Semglee) — first interchangeable biosimilar insulin
expected_details.practical_meaning: pharmacist can substitute without prescriber intervention (like generic drugs)
expected_details.distinction: interchangeable > biosimilar (biosimilar requires prescriber specification; interchangeable allows automatic substitution)

input: Does clozapine have a REMS? What are the requirements?

expected_details.key_requirement: mandatory ANC (absolute neutrophil count) monitoring — risk of severe neutropenia/agranulocytosis
expected_details.dispensing_restriction: cannot dispense without current ANC result in system

input: Explain pediatric exclusivity for drugs. How much additional exclusivity does it grant? Does it apply to Prozac (fluoxetine)?

expected_details.exclusivity_duration: 6 months additional exclusivity added to all existing patents and exclusivities
expected_details.mechanism: FDA issues Written Request → sponsor conducts pediatric study → if adequate, 6 months granted
expected_details.fluoxetine_pediatric: yes — fluoxetine has pediatric indication (MDD in children ≥8, OCD in children ≥7)
expected_details.distinction_from_prea: pediatric exclusivity is voluntary (incentive-based); PREA is mandatory
